sources.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Overarching theme covering construction of mathematical arguments, diagrams, graphs and precise symbolic language.
Broad requirement on mathematical language and syntax; maps to precise use of symbols.
Set-theoretic language and symbols applied to inequalities and probability.
Definition of a function and its domain/range.
Comprehending and critiquing arguments, proofs and justifications.
Recognising mathematical structure and abstracting situations.
Extended arguments for unstructured/contextual problems.
Interpreting and communicating solutions in context; closest node concerns interpreting outputs.
General statement about numerical methods; no single technique-specific node covers the idea.
Evaluating accuracy/limitations including reasoned estimates.

syllabusPoints.unmappedReason: No canonical node represents the problem-solving cycle as a whole; available nodes cover only sub-skills.
reviewNotes: Problem-solving cycle concept not matched by current catalog.
Using and constructing diagrams, including in mechanics.
Translating a contextual situation into a model with simplifying assumptions.
Using a model with suitable inputs to explore situations.
Interpreting model outputs in the original context.
Refining models and evaluating appropriateness; broader than individual cycle nodes.
Understanding and using modelling assumptions.

syllabusPoints.sourceReference: p.14, Use of data in statistics

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification
evidence.locator: p.14, Use of data in statistics

reviewNotes: Official requirement to study the pre-release large data set; assessed within Paper 3 Statistics.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Single specification row covering all four required proof methods, including contradiction proofs for sqrt(2) irrationality and infinity of primes.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Laws of indices for all rational exponents and the equivalence of a^(m/n) and n-th root of a^m.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Use, simplification and rationalising of surds.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Broad point covering quadratics: discriminant, completing the square, solving by several methods and equations quadratic in a function.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Simultaneous equations in two variables, including one linear and one quadratic equation.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Linear and quadratic inequalities, graphical interpretation, set notation and graph shading.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Polynomial algebraic manipulation, factor theorem, division by linear expressions and simplification of rational expressions.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Covers sketching polynomial, modulus, reciprocal and proportional graphs; interpreting equations via intersections; proportional relationships.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Composite and inverse functions, domain/range, one-one/many-one mappings and reflection property of inverse graphs.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Transformations of y=f(x): translations, stretches, reflections and combinations, including modulus graphs.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Partial fractions with denominators up to three linear factors or repeated linear factors; applications to integration/series.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Use of functions in modelling and consideration of model limitations/refinements.

syllabusPoints.sourceReference: p.20, Topic 3 Coordinate geometry in the (x,y) plane, 3.1

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification
evidence.locator: p.20, Topic 3 Coordinate geometry in the (x,y) plane, 3.1

reviewNotes: Equation of a straight line in various forms, parallel/perpendicular conditions and straight-line modelling.

syllabusPoints.sourceReference: p.20, Topic 3 Coordinate geometry in the (x,y) plane, 3.2

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification
evidence.locator: p.20, Topic 3 Coordinate geometry in the (x,y) plane, 3.2

reviewNotes: Circle equations, centre and radius, circle properties and tangents/normal lines.

syllabusPoints.sourceReference: p.21, Topic 3 Coordinate geometry in the (x,y) plane, 3.3

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification
evidence.locator: p.21, Topic 3 Coordinate geometry in the (x,y) plane, 3.3

reviewNotes: Parametric equations of curves and conversion between Cartesian and parametric forms, including parameter domain.

syllabusPoints.sourceReference: p.21, Topic 3 Coordinate geometry in the (x,y) plane, 3.4

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification
evidence.locator: p.21, Topic 3 Coordinate geometry in the (x,y) plane, 3.4

reviewNotes: Parametric equations used in modelling contexts; may also connect to kinematics in Paper 3.
Paper 3 eligibility added for the explicit kinematics cross-reference.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Binomial expansion for positive integer n and rational n, coefficients, Pascal's triangle, validity condition and approximation.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Sequences defined by nth term formula or recurrence relation, including increasing/decreasing/periodic behaviour.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: No dedicated sigma-notation node in the supplied catalog; mapped to the parent Series node.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Arithmetic sequences and series, nth term and sum to n terms, including proof of sum formula.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Geometric sequences and series, finite sum, sum to infinity for |r|<1, and use of logarithms to find n.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Modelling with sequences and series, e.g. savings schemes.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Covers trig ratios for all arguments, sine/cosine rules, area formula with ambiguous case, and radian measure for arcs/sectors.

syllabusPoints.unmappedReason: No canonical node in the supplied catalog covers small-angle approximations of sin, cos and tan.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Small-angle approximations in radians are required but no matching node exists.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Sine, cosine and tangent functions: graphs, symmetries, periodicity and exact values.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Secant, cosecant, cotangent and inverse trigonometric functions, including graphs, domains and ranges.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Fundamental trigonometric identities and derived sec/cosec identities used for solving equations and proving identities.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Compound-angle and double-angle formulae; also a cos theta + b sin theta in R-form, which has no dedicated node.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Solving trigonometric equations in a given interval, including quadratic and multiple-angle equations.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Constructing proofs involving trigonometric functions and identities.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Applying trigonometry to contextual problems including vectors, kinematics and forces.
Includes graph shapes for a<1 and a>1 and transformed graph y=e^(ax+b)+c not fully captured by nodes.
Combines derivative of e^(kx) with suitability of exponential models when rate of change is proportional to y.
Covers logarithm as inverse of a^x, natural log, and solving exponential/logarithmic equations.
Machine-generated candidate; independent point review required.
Subset of exponential and logarithmic equations; change of base may be used.
Log-log and log-linear plots to estimate parameters; no dedicated canonical node for this linearisation technique.
Includes modelling contexts, limitations, refinements and behaviour for large t.
Spans derivative concepts, first principles for x^n/sin x/cos x, sketching gradient functions, second derivatives and points of inflection.
Includes rational powers of x, e^(kx), a^(kx), sin kx, cos kx, tan kx and derivative of ln x.
Applications to gradients, tangents, normals, stationary points, points of inflection and increasing/decreasing functions.
Includes product, quotient and chain rules, connected rates of change, inverse functions and derivatives of cosec x, sec x and cot x.
First derivative only for implicit and parametric curves; equations of tangents and normals required.
Constructing first-order differential equations in pure mathematics and in context.
Machine-generated candidate; independent point review required.
Includes integration using trigonometric identities and finding curve equations from f'(x) and a point.
Includes areas bounded by curves and straight lines, and between curves including parametric curves.
No dedicated Riemann-sum node; mapped to definite integral concept.
Includes integrals of form f'(x)/f(x) and integral of ln x.
Integration using partial fractions with linear denominators.
Analytical solution of first-order separable differential equations including particular solutions.
Interpretation of differential equation solutions in context including limitations and kinematic links.
Machine-generated candidate; independent point review required.
Machine-generated candidate; independent point review required.
Machine-generated candidate; independent point review required.
Machine-generated candidate; independent point review required.
Broad application of numerical methods in context; mapped to numerical methods root-finding parent only.
Machine-generated candidate; independent point review required.
Machine-generated candidate; independent point review required.
Machine-generated candidate; independent point review required.
Distance between points in 2D and 3D linked to position vectors.
Vector problem solving in pure mathematics and mechanics contexts including forces.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Includes population/sample, sampling techniques and critiquing samples; not all named sampling techniques have dedicated nodes.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Histogram area represents frequency; also references frequency polygons not separately mapped.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Regression-line calculations are excluded; includes log transformation for power/exponential relationships.

evidence.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

reviewNotes: Includes central tendency, spread, coding, linear interpolation for percentiles and standard deviation from summary statistics.
Covers outlier recognition, critique of data presentations and data cleaning; mapped to summary-statistic and statistical-diagram nodes because no single dedicated data-cleaning node exists.
Mutually exclusive and independent events plus links to discrete/continuous distributions; mapped to probability-rule and distribution-model nodes.
Conditional probability using tree/Venn/two-way tables and the conditional formula; mapped to conditional probability and supporting rule/diagram nodes.
Probability modelling including critiquing assumptions and effects of more realistic assumptions; mapped to modelling-cycle nodes.
Simple discrete distributions and the binomial model; calculation of general discrete mean/variance is excluded; mapped to DRV and binomial nodes.
Normal distribution, its properties, links to histograms/spread/inflection points and normal approximation to binomial; mapped to normal and binomial-normal nodes plus supporting data nodes.
Selecting an appropriate probability distribution with reasoning; mapped to modelling-assumption and distribution-model nodes.
Hypothesis-testing language developed through a binomial model, and PMCC interpretation; mapped to hypothesis-test concept nodes and the product-moment-correlation node.
Binomial proportion hypothesis test with inference about population and significance-level meaning; mapped to binomial hypothesis-test and sampling/population nodes.
Normal mean hypothesis test with known/given/assumed variance; mapped to mean-test and sample-mean-distribution nodes.
